author: Wa
body:
Glad to hear good review, I’m going to the Melbourne gig. :)  
Can you tell me how long the show lasted with two opening acts?  
Thanks

author: Burgo
body:
Hi Wa,  
Thanks for stopping by… hope you have a blast at the Melbourne gig. In answer to your question, the doors opened at 8pm at the Troubadour, and I think the first opening act came on at around 8.35 or so… and we left the Troubadour just before midnight, although we did stick around for a while to meet Howie afterwards; so I would think that the last song probably finished around 11.20pm or so.

author: Sam
body: Hey man, great review. I saw Howie last night in Melbourne at the East Brunswick Club, which I suppose must be a similar size to the Troubadour. Simply awesome show, great setlist too. We missed out on Madrigals, which had me gutted, but No Longer What You Require was an absolute highlight! Here’s hoping he doesn’t mess with that beautiful acoustic arrangement on the album version. I think the only other changes for us was the addition of Morning After (sooo good) and a couple of covers: The Drugs Don’t Work, and a 2nd encore of Don’t Dream It’s Over, which turned into a nice singalong. Sophie Koh was incredible too, hadn’t really heard of her before last night and I’m already tracking down her albums. Did she play Creep for you guys? What a voice!

author: Burgo
body:
Hi Sam,  
Thanks for leaving a comment! Sounds like you guys had a great show… I heard that Howie had started including “Don’t Dream It’s Over”, which I’m pretty bummed he didn’t do at the Brisbane show!  
And yes, Sophie did play “Creep” in her set… I meant to actually mention it in the review, it was a great cover.

Final point: yeah, I totally agree with you re: No Longer What You Require. I really hope Howie keeps the sparse acoustic arrangement if it goes on the next album.

Thanks again for stopping by Sam, it’s much appreciated.

author: Mike
body:
Hi guys,

I caught Howie in Ballarat on the 3rd of december. It was incredible. Anyway, just so you know… He included “Don’t dream it’s over” and also “The drugs don’t work” by The Verve.  
Also.. check out a young guy from counrty vic. On tour with Josh Pyke atm. His name is Jackson McLaren. He was amazing too!

author: Burgo
body:
Hi Mike,  
Thanks for stopping by the post. “The Drugs Don’t Work” was another cover I was hoping to hear, looks like subsequent shows really lucked out. Did Jackson McLaren open for Howie?

author: Andrew
body: Hey, I was also at the Melbourne show and yes, Jackson McLaren opened for Howie, though I only caught the last two songs due to having a bit of trouble finding parking at the venue hahaha.

author: Burgo
body:
Hey Andrew,  
Thanks for letting me know. I’ll definitely be looking into checking out Jackson McLaren in the future!

author: Burgo
body:
Ed’s note:  
As a treat to those of you who have made it this far down… I’ve just seen that someone has uploaded _part_ (unfortunately not the whole thing) of Howie’s performance of the new song mentioned above, “No Longer What You Require” from his Melbourne show.

I’ve embedded it below. As I say, unfortunately it’s not the whole thing… but it gives you a taste of just how damn cool this song is.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/7alxx7z7M_I" title="YouTube video" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen=""></iframe>

author: Sam
body: Haha, yes, that video was made by my buddy Rae Min, who was standing next to me up the front. He was kicking himself after the show for not recording the whole thing, but he’s got his hands on a full video of the Brisbane rendition, so perhaps that’ll get up on YouTube before long!

author: Burgo
body: Hah… small world Sam! I really hope he posts the Brisbane performance then (obviously, seeing as it was the show that I was at :P)… be sure to drop a line if it does go up on the ‘Tube!

author: Sam
body:
Hi again,

I’ve put the full Troubadour version of ‘No Longer What You Require’ up on YouTube myself, so here it is.

[http://au.youtube.com/watch?v=yxtZEsf7LuY](http://au.youtube.com/watch?v=yxtZEsf7LuY)

Enjoy!

\*\*\*\*

Ed’s Note: See [Peter’s comment below](/2008/11/28/howie-day-live-at-the-troubadour-in-brisbane/#comment-5380) clarifying this

author: Burgo
body:
Thanks Sam, that’s much appreciated… replaying the video for the third time as we speak :)

For those who are too lazy to click through to your link, I’ve embedded the video below:

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/yxtZEsf7LuY" title="YouTube video" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen=""></iframe>

author: Peter
body: That wasn’t the Troubadour, it was Annandale. I know because it was my video.

author: Burgo
body:
Hi Peter,

Thanks for that… I thought to myself when Sam said it was the Troubs’ show, “Hmmm, I don’t remember the backdrop looking like that at all”. Good to know I wasn’t imagining it!

And thanks for taking such an amazing video, it’s much appreciated!

author: Mike
body: If anyone has managed to tab/chord the two new songs (Longest Night, Not What You Require) Please let me know asap!

author: Linda
body:
Hello!  
I love this entire review! I’ve been a huge fan of Howie for years and will finally see my first show with foot pedals instead of a band next weekend in Minneapolis and again in Milwaukee. Can’t wait!  
I watched his Annandale show online and fell in love with “No Longer What You Require” and wanted to sing alone with the lyrics so, thanks for that! I think that the question mark you have in the song is “thrills”. Is the opening act is any good?

author: Jesse
body:
I do know how to play “no longer what you require” and  
“the longest night”. I’m sure i could send you a tutorial on how to them both if you have not already learned them. Also i wanted to say howie changes the way thhe chorus begins in no longer what you require, sometimes he will say ” I could of told you that i love you.” and other times he will replace the word “could” with “would”.

author: Burgo
body:
Hi Jesse,  
Thanks for stopping by with that offer in reply to Mike’s question. If you’d like you could send through the tutorial to me and I could then pass on the tutorial to Mike (seeing as I’m unsure he would be happy for me to give out his email without his permission). Alternatively, feel free to link through to the tutorial for the songs if you have them uploaded anywhere :)

author: mike
body: Hi jesse & Burgo. That would be muchly appreciated if I could get those chords etc sent to me! Thanks alot.

author: Burgo
body:
Hi Mike,  
If Jesse emails me the details, I’ll pass on to you as soon as I get them. Otherwise, hopefully Jesse will post a link here, so keep checking back to see if the comments are updated!

author: Kreg
body: This is a late response, but if you still have the tutorial of “No Longer What You Require,” I’d love for you to send it to my email. I’ve searched the internet for a while and haven’t found any tabs for it.

author: mike
body: It never got to me anyway!

author: Burgo
body:
Hi Kreg and Mike,  
Yep, unfortunately Jesse never dropped me that email, or a link to the chords, so sadly I can’t point you in the right direction… if someone else out there can held Kreg and Mike, let us know!

author: mia
body: i like howie day his song!! :D right now i’m listening longest night♫
